title: rubus × horstensis
intro: Rubus × horstensis, also known as the Horst's black raspberry, is a hybrid plant species resulting from the cross between Rubus occidentalis and Rubus allegheniensis, both members of the rose family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × horstensis include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and support cognitive function. Herbal preparations of Rubus × horstensis can be found in the form of jams, jellies, teas, and supplements, often used to support overall well-being and promote a healthy lifestyle.
